file.provenance: LOPS_maize_P1.xlsx
MaizeField3D
MaizeField3D + 2 prepended stub positions (length_scales=[0.45, 0.65], width_scales=[0.6, 0.8])
MaizeField3D PLY point clouds
Fournier & Andrieu 2000, Ann Bot 86:551-563, Fig 12 panels A and B
Desktop/PHD_Papers_Markdown/Fournier - 2000.../_page_9_Figure_9.jpeg
Vision extraction from printed figure with overlapping markers and CI bars; ±10% global. Tighten to ±2% via WebPlotDigitizer on a high-res rescan if needed.

Birch 2002 line 173 reports Déa phyt-9 Phase III rate = 0.38 cm/°Cd, exactly matching the vision-extracted value. Independent paper, independent fit, exact match → at least the peak rank has ~0% error. Other ranks remain at ±10% pending per-rank Birch cross-check (Layer D.2 minor follow-up).
Déa
Andrieu, Hillier & Birch (2006) Annals of Botany 98:1005-1016
Hillier, Makowski & Andrieu (2005) Annals of Botany 96:137-148
Dea (temperate French dent), Grignon, normal density 9.5 plants m^-2
figure-read from Figs 6, 7, 9, 10 of Andrieu 2006

Vision-read approximations of Figs 6 (R1), 7 (lag_exp), 9 (R2), 10 (D_lin). +/-15% global. For publication-grade precision use Vidal 2021 SupData3 (SupData3-fit_3phase_models_s2.xlsx, PMC7877697) for M40/M52 inbred-derived alternates, or request original Hillier-et-al. ML fits.

R2_cm_per_Cd_published values from artifact (~2-6 cm/Cd) are systematically ~10x the kinematically realistic LER for maize (Tardieu/Reymond, Birch report 0.2-1.0 cm/Cd). Likely a unit-label mismatch in the figure-read source (mm/Cd printed as cm/Cd) or a figure-axis misread. Carried verbatim for traceability; superseded by R2_cm_per_Cd_rescaled which is anchored on MF3D L_fin via C^1 continuity and lands in the kinematic literature range.
Tb=9.8 used for both leaf and stem clocks per ADR D3. Andrieu 2006 publishes Tb=9.7; difference within fitting CI. Single Tb avoids artificial offsets between leaf and stem submodels.

T0_Cd here is the Andrieu phase-E origin on the Tb=9.8 axis (M7), NOT the leaf birth gate. The birth gate is ldelay + ldelayAxis=TT on LeafRandomParameter (post-Lock #8, CPlantBox ff30912e, S0.9). Bake script must satisfy T0_Cd >= ldelay per leaf subType: a leaf cannot enter Andrieu phase E before it is born. Validation gate active once XML is baked with axis="TT" ldelay values.

R2_cm_per_Cd_rescaled is computed under C^1 continuity (Andrieu 2006 eq. C.3) with R1 and D_lin held at published figure-read values, anchoring L_fin on MF3D lmax_n at the corresponding position (per ADR D2). Sanity flag R2_rescale_flag fires when the rescaled-to-published ratio is outside [0.7, 1.3] (per ADR C5). Formula: L1 = L_fin_MF3D / (1 + R1 * D_lin); R2_rescaled = R1 * L1.

Ranks 16-17 linearly extrapolated from rank 13-15 slope per ADR C4: R1 slope = -0.005/rank; R2_published slope = -1.0/rank; lag_exp constant plateau at 150 Cd; D_lin slope = -20/rank with floor 15 Cd to avoid degeneracy. Mirrors stem-side phase_III_per_rank.json extrapolation pattern. lag_exp_Cd_implied diverges sharply (>250 Cd) for these ranks because MF3D lmax at top positions (44-50 cm) is much larger than Dea flag-leaf lengths.

T0_Cd schedule per Vidal et al. 2021 (Ann. Bot. Plants, doi:10.1093/aobpla/plaa072 — same Andrieu lab as AHB 2006, M52/M40 cultivars derived from MBS847). Vidal Fig 3 + Table 1 report a bilinear plastochron: leaves 1-9 initiated at 19.7 °Cd Tb=10 (lower regime, heterotrophic→early autotrophic), leaves 10+ initiated at 32.9 °Cd Tb=10 (upper regime, post-juvenile-adult phase change). Padilla & Otegui 2005 (Ann. Bot. 96:997-1007) corroborate this biological breakpoint via a bilinear N_AL/N_IL coordination model fitted on 16 US/Argentina hybrids: breakpoint at ~8 initiated primordia matches the he

R1_Cd_inv per-rank values are AHB 2006 figure-reads (Déa, French dent), the only published rank-resolved R1 dataset complete enough to use directly. Vidal 2021 Fig 5A reports a U-shaped RER pattern (high at ranks 4-9, minimum at rank 10-11, increasing for ranks 12+) which differs from AHB's monotonic-decreasing assumption — but Vidal's per-rank R1 values aren't tabulated to the precision needed for direct substitution. Treat the U-shape as a structural claim from Vidal and the numerical R1 values as AHB-Déa best-available. If a US-dent rank-resolved R1 dataset becomes available, swap values in

Leaf elongation kinetics are anchored to appearance time via the leaf's tt_emergence parameter (Tb=8 axis on the plant), not to AHB's primordium initiation (Tb=9.8 Andrieu axis). The 30 °Cd tip-emergence-to-appearance interval reported by Vidal 2021 (constant across ranks) is therefore implicit in the appearance-clock calibration of tt_emergence and is NOT applied as an explicit offset in MultiPhaseLeafGrowth. Our XML tt_emergence ≈ 48 °Cd Tb=10 equivalent at T=20°C lands within Padilla & Otegui 2005's US-dent phyllochron range (48.6-65.5 Cd Tb=2.9-5.0 across 16 hybrids) and within ~14% of Vid

After reverting the rank-5/8/9 R1 retunes (May 2026, see git log) and adopting the Vidal bilinear T0, the Nielsen V4/V6 calendar drift exceeds the ±2 d test gate. This residual is intentionally left visible: it is the signature of unmodulated leaf kinetics under field met (no carbon/water modulation), and is the validation target for Ch2 coupling. Test test_fa_vstage_calendar.py marks V4/V6 xfail with Ch2 TODO.

Per ADR C4: positions 0-1 of our 16-leaf XML are FA-S3b stubs (project_16leaf_extension memory) and represent Dea juvenile ranks 1-3 (all gated). Positions 2-15 map to Dea ranks 4-17 1:1 (rank n -> position n-2). Cultivar offset between MF3D peak (position 6) and Dea peak (rank 11) is absorbed by the per-rank R2 rescaling.

In maize_calibrated.xml, <leaf subType="N"/> uses N = Dea rank directly (subType 2 = stub for rank 2, ..., subType 17 = rank 17). The bake script writes per-rank arrays into the matching subType; gated ranks (1-3) get zero-valued kinetic fields.
wheat_parameterextraction_scans
Zhu et al. 2014, Ann Bot 113:1217-1228, Supplementary Methods S1 + Fig S2
Desktop/PHD_Papers_Markdown/Zhu et al. - 2014.../mcu051supp/mcu051supp.md line 9-12
combined: Déa NS 1996 + 2000, Déa HD 2000, Nobilis NS 1998 + 2000
fit spans Déa + Nobilis cultivars at multiple densities; ratio shape held across these

constant.name: GDD_MATURITY
constant.citation: ~1600 GDD from sowing to physiological maturity

constant.name: TMAX_MAIZE
constant.citation: upper cutoff — no extra GDD above this

constant.name: CELL_SIZE
constant.citation: meters (not directly settable via simple API; scene.size controls it)

constant.name: DEFAULT_N_CP
constant.citation: number of control points per deformation type

constant.name: MIN_SKELETON_SPACING
constant.citation: cm — resample denser skeletons to this

constant.name: MIN_WIDTH
constant.citation: cm — clamp widths below this (prevents zero-area tips)

constant.name: N_STRUCTURAL
constant.citation: lmax, Width_blade, theta, tropismS, tropismAge, r, areaMax

constant.name: SENESCENCE_S_BEND_R1
constant.citation: hinge at ~1/3 arc length at onset

constant.name: SENESCENCE_S_BEND_R4
constant.citation: hinge walks slightly distal by end-phase

constant.name: SENESCENCE_DROOP_BASE_DEG
constant.citation: no immediate snap; pitch ramps from 0

constant.name: _PROFILE_SHAPE_FACTOR
constant.citation: trapezoidal integral of typical maize profile

constant.name: VEG_F760_MIN
constant.citation: W/m2/sr/um — above MC scatter (~1e-4), below canopy core (>1e-3)

constant.name: RATIO_F760_MIN
constant.citation: same as VEG for ratio maps (previous 1e-4 too loose)

constant.name: TARGET_PAR_UMOL
constant.citation: µmol/m²/s — used to normalise Phase 1 APAR

location.citation: µmol/m²/s — used to normalise Phase 1 APAR
